name: Redbone
description:
'''Redbone''' is an American [[rock music|rock]] band that was originally formed in [[Los Angeles]], California, in 1969 by brothers [[Pat Vegas|Pat]] and [[Lolly Vegas]]. All members during their commercial peak were of [[Chicano]] and [[Native Americans in the United States|Native American]] heritage, something heavily reflected in their songs, stage regalia, and album art. Catching fire in the 1970s, the band broke boundaries in the realm of rock music and shined a light on social issues while topping the charts.

Their released albums consist of ''Redbone'' (1970), ''[[Potlatch (album)|Potlatch]]'' (1970), ''[[The Witch Queen of New Orleans]]'' (1971), ''[[Already Here]]'' (1972), ''[[Wovoka#In popular culture|Wovoka]]'' (1973), ''[[Beaded Dreams Through Turquoise Eyes]]'' (1974), ''Soliloquy in Red'' (1975), ''[[Cycles (Redbone album)|Cycles]]'' (1977), and ''[[Peace Pipe (Redbone album)|Peace Pipe]]'' (2005). 

They made the [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] in the spring of 1974 with "[[Come and Get Your Love]]". The [[Single (music)|single]] was certified gold, selling over a million copies. Redbone became the first Native American band to have such success, with the song reaching number 5. Redbone got additional notice in America with the singles "We Were All Wounded at Wounded Knee", "[[The Witch Queen of New Orleans]]", "Wovoka", and "Maggie", although these were more successful overseas.

Pat has been the sole consistent member of the band since Lolly's death in 2010.
